reforms.description: In 2001, the Greek government initiated a new health care reform programme aimed at reorganising services and establishing new mechanisms for efficient resource use and high-quality health care provision, including the decentralisation of decision-making authority through the establishment of 17 Regional Health Systems.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reform aims to improve health care efficiency and quality, which can enhance overall productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been put into practice as of 2001.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant structural change in the health care system.
reforms.source_quote: In 2001, the government initiated a new health care reform programme with an implementation horizon of 6 years.

reforms.description: Greece has made progress in strengthening the links between universities and enterprises through initiatives that support the establishment of spin-off firms, aiming to enhance R&D and innovation programmes.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing collaboration between universities and enterprises can lead to increased innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiatives have been successfully implemented as of 2002.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiatives represent a significant step towards improving knowledge transfer but are not as comprehensive as major reforms.
reforms.source_quote: Some progress towards strengthening the links between universities and enterprises through initiatives supporting the establishment of spin-off firms.

reforms.description: Greece has initiated an ambitious programme to combat digital illiteracy in schools, which includes setting up IT laboratories, developing educational software, and training teachers, with the goal of connecting all schools to the Internet by the end of 2002.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving digital literacy among teachers and students enhances the overall skill level of the workforce, supporting innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been successfully implemented as of 2002.
reforms.importance_rationale: The programme represents a significant investment in human capital but is not a structural reform.
reforms.source_quote: Greece has engaged in an ambitious programme to combat digital illiteracy in schools, combining the setting up of IT laboratories, the development of educational software and the training of a large number of teachers.

reforms.description: The OPIS is an innovative horizontal programme with a total cost of €2.8 billion aimed at equipping and networking schools and universities, supporting entrepreneurship, and promoting the digital economy.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investing in research infrastructure supports innovation and entrepreneurship, which can drive economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been successfully implemented as of 2000.
reforms.importance_rationale: The programme represents a major investment in research infrastructure.
reforms.source_quote: The OPIS is an innovative horizontal programme, including actions which fall in four broad areas: education and culture; citizens and quality of life; employment and social inclusion; and actions related to the new economy.
